subprocess_flow.description: The initial step involves identifying all potential data sources relevant to the AI model's objectives. This includes internal databases, external APIs, data lakes, and any other repositories containing information that could be valuable for training or usage. A comprehensive understanding of available data is crucial for ensuring the model's accuracy and effectiveness.
success_criteria: Assessment of data source relevance and accessibility
subprocess_flow.purpose: To identify all relevant data sources for AI model training and usage.
subprocess_flow.scope: All internal and external data repositories.

step_risks_and_controls.control: Conduct thorough data source discovery and documentation.

subprocess_flow.description: Once the data sources are identified, the next step is to extract the raw data from these sources. This involves using appropriate tools and techniques to retrieve the data in its original format. The extraction process should be efficient and reliable, ensuring that all necessary data is obtained without errors or omissions. This step may involve setting up data pipelines or using APIs to access the data.
success_criteria: Successful extraction of raw data from identified sources
Complete and accurate data extraction logs
subprocess_flow.purpose: To extract raw data from identified sources for further processing.
subprocess_flow.scope: Data extraction from all identified data sources.

step_risks_and_controls.control: Implement data validation and error handling procedures.

subprocess_flow.description: This critical step involves cleaning and transforming the raw data to ensure its quality and suitability for AI model training. Cleaning includes handling missing values, removing outliers, and correcting inconsistencies. Transformation involves converting data into a suitable format, such as scaling numerical features or encoding categorical variables. High-quality data is essential for building accurate and reliable AI models.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To clean and transform raw data into a suitable format for AI model training.
subprocess_flow.scope: Data cleaning and transformation for all extracted data.

step_risks_and_controls.control: Implement data validation and cleaning procedures.

subprocess_flow.description: For supervised learning models, labeling the data is a crucial step. This involves assigning appropriate labels or categories to the data points, enabling the model to learn the relationship between the input features and the desired output. Accurate and consistent labeling is essential for ensuring the model's predictive performance. This step may involve manual labeling by domain experts or automated labeling using existing knowledge bases.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To label the data for supervised learning models.
subprocess_flow.scope: Data labeling for all relevant data points.

step_risks_and_controls.control: Implement data validation and quality control procedures.

subprocess_flow.description: The final step is to validate the prepared dataset to ensure its quality and suitability for AI model training. This involves performing various checks and tests to verify the data's accuracy, completeness, and consistency. Validation may include statistical analysis, data profiling, and manual review. Addressing any issues identified during validation is crucial for building reliable AI models.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To validate the prepared dataset for AI model training.
subprocess_flow.scope: Data validation for the entire dataset.

step_risks_and_controls.control: Implement data validation and quality control procedures.
